```text
160	// RESERVED SEAT POOLS (Gov/Hospital + Senior/PWD) — constants + utilities
161	// See spec:
162	// docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-reserved-seat-pools-design.md
163	// ============================================================================
164	const GOV_POOL_PER_CLASS = 5;
165	const SENIOR_PWD_POOL_PER_CLASS = 4;
166	const PASSENGER_TYPE_GOV_HOSPITAL = 'Gov/Hospital';
167	
168	// Returns 'regular' | 'govHospital' | 'seniorPwd' | null for sold out.
169	// Pure function — does NOT mutate `pools`. Caller is responsible for applying
170	// the consumption (incrementing `taken` or `pending`) once it accepts the result.
171	const consumePool = (pools, passengerType) => {
172	  if (!pools) return null;
173	  const reg = pools.regular || { capacity: 0, taken: 0 };
174	  const gov = pools.govHospital || { capacity: 0, taken: 0, pending: 0 };
175	  const sp  = pools.seniorPwd || { capacity: 0, taken: 0 };
176	  const regAvail = reg.taken < reg.capacity;
177	  const govAvail = (gov.taken + (gov.pending || 0)) < gov.capacity;
178	  const spAvail  = sp.taken < sp.capacity;
179	
180	  if (passengerType === PASSENGER_TYPE_GOV_HOSPITAL) {
181	    return govAvail ? 'govHospital' : null;
182	  }
183	  if (passengerType === 'Senior' || passengerType === 'PWD') {
184	    if (spAvail) return 'seniorPwd';
185	    if (regAvail) return 'regular'; // Senior/PWD keeps 20% discount
186	    return null; // never spills into Gov/Hospital
187	  }
188	  // Regular passenger (Adult / Student / Child / Infant)
189	  if (regAvail) return 'regular';
190	  if (govAvail) return 'govHospital';
191	  if (spAvail)  return 'seniorPwd';
192	  return null;
193	};
194	
195	// Maps a seat label (e.g. "A03-B") to which pool slice it belongs to, based on
196	// the seat grid for the class. Last GOV_POOL_PER_CLASS seats of the class are
197	// the Gov/Hospital pool; the SENIOR_PWD_POOL_PER_CLASS seats before that are
198	// the Senior/PWD pool; everything else is regular. This is the seat-picker's
199	// visual contract — it does not affect the consumePool cascade.
200	const poolForSeat = (seatLabel, classCapacity) => {
201	  if (!seatLabel) return 'regular';
202	  // Seats are ordered row-major; assume seatIndex is derivable from the label.
203	  // For mockup purposes we just take a numeric tail to assign pools.
204	  const m = seatLabel.match(/(\d+)-([A-Z])/);
205	  if (!m) return 'regular';
206	  const row = parseInt(m[1], 10);
207	  const col = m[2].charCodeAt(0) - 'A'.charCodeAt(0);
208	  // Pseudo seat index: (row-1) * 8 + col is good enough to bucket the tail.
209	  const idx = (row - 1) * 8 + col;
210	  const govStart = classCapacity - GOV_POOL_PER_CLASS;
211	  const spStart  = classCapacity - GOV_POOL_PER_CLASS - SENIOR_PWD_POOL_PER_CLASS;
212	  if (idx >= govStart) return 'govHospital';
213	  if (idx >= spStart)  return 'seniorPwd';
214	  return 'regular';
215	};
216	
217	// Build the per-class `pools` object for a given total class capacity.
218	// Pure helper used by sailing seed data.
219	const buildPools = (classCapacity) => ({
220	  regular:     { capacity: Math.max(0, classCapacity - GOV_POOL_PER_CLASS - SENIOR_PWD_POOL_PER_CLASS), taken: 0 },
221	  govHospital: { capacity: GOV_POOL_PER_CLASS, taken: 0, pending: 0 },
222	  seniorPwd:   { capacity: SENIOR_PWD_POOL_PER_CLASS, taken: 0 },
223	});
224	
225	// ============================================================================
226	// ReservedPoolBadge — small chip used in seat pickers, manifests, and the
227	// walk-in daily list to mark seats/passengers belonging to a reserved pool.
228	// ============================================================================
229	function ReservedPoolBadge({ pool, size = 'sm' }) {
230	  if (pool === 'regular' || !pool) return null;
231	  const palette = pool === 'govHospital'
232	    ? { bg: '#E9D5FF', fg: '#5B21B6', label: 'GOV/HOSPITAL POOL', short: 'GH' }
233	    : { bg: '#FEF3C7', fg: '#92400E', label: 'SENIOR/PWD POOL', short: 'SP' };
234	  if (size === 'xs') {
235	    return (
236	      <span
237	        className="inline-block rounded font-bold leading-none px-1 text-[7px]"
238	        style={{ background: palette.bg, color: palette.fg }}
239	      >
240	        {palette.short}
241	      </span>
242	    );
243	  }
244	  const padding = size === 'sm' ? 'px-1.5 py-0.5' : 'px-2 py-1';
245	  const text = size === 'sm' ? 'text-[9px]' : 'text-[11px]';
246	  return (
247	    <span
248	      className={`inline-block rounded font-bold tracking-wide ${padding} ${text}`}
249	      style={{ background: palette.bg, color: palette.fg }}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 06:46:08Z)**_

All five deferred follow-ups wired and verified:

**Changes** (all in `docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`):
- Added `seatsAvailableInClass(classPools)` helper — derives available count across regular + govHospital (minus pending) + seniorPwd slices.
- Lifted `sailings` (with per-class `pools`) to `FandSMarineMockup` root `useState`; passed to both `StaffWalkinScreen` and `AdminGovHospitalApprovalsScreen`.
- Dropped hardcoded `seats: { openair, aircon, vip }` on sailings; the OA/AC/VIP chips in walk-in step 1 now read live from pools.
- `handleConfirm` runs `consumePool` per passenger on a cloned pool to plan allocations, blocks on cascade sold-out, then mutates `setSailings` — Gov/Hospital → `pending++`, regular/Senior/PWD → `taken++` on the resolved slice.
- `approve(ref)`: locates booking's sailing+class, `govHospital.pending--`, `govHospital.taken++`.
- `confirmReject()`: `govHospital.pending--` only.
- Both admin handlers no-op on bookings already in terminal state (idempotent).

Want me to commit these, or test in-browser first?

---

```md
---

Two FerryBook mockup feature specs brainstormed and approved on 2026-05-29 by user; committed in `39af5b8`. Both shipped to `main`. All edits live in `docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`.

**Specs (both IMPLEMENTED, both with follow-ups closed):**

- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-vessel-scoped-report-viewer-roles-design.md` — IMPLEMENTED 2026-05-30. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-29-vessel-scoped-report-viewer-roles.md`. 14 tasks, commits `5cc95f3..657f7b6`. Three follow-ups landed in `5bf5bcd` (portFilter compound, multi-vessel KPI scale, navigator highlight).

- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-reserved-seat-pools-design.md` — IMPLEMENTED 2026-05-30. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-30-reserved-seat-pools.md`. 16 commits, `30535ad..6538bfe` (14 tasks + 2 mid-task fixes). All 7 deferred follow-ups closed later 2026-05-30 in commits `29b2de6` + `0d1429e`:
  1. `consumePool` wired into `StaffWalkinScreen.handleConfirm` — runs per passenger against cloned pool, blocks on sold-out cascade, then mutates `setSailings` (Gov/Hospital → `pending++`, regular/Senior/PWD → `taken++` on resolved slice).
  2. Admin approve commits `pending--` + `taken++`; reject releases `pending--`. `sailings` lifted to `FandSMarineMockup` root so walk-in + admin share one source of truth.
  3. Pool utilization KPI denominator now derived live from `sailings.pools` (sum capacity vs taken+pending across non-departed sailings); label renamed to "Pool utilization (upcoming)".
  4. `dateFilter` state — still captured-but-unused as of 0d1429e (low priority; not addressed in this batch).
  5. `approvedToday`/`rejectedToday` now scoped via `submittedAt.startsWith('May 30')` prefix check.
  6. Boarding officer duplicated JSX extracted to `GovHospitalGangwayNote` component.
  7. Voyage-departure guard: approve/openReject/confirmReject short-circuit when `sailings.find(s => s.id === b.sailingId)?.departed`; row shows `Lock`-icon "Voyage departed" chip instead of action buttons.

**Why:** User wanted "small additional feature" mid-flow (Gov/Hospital seats) which expanded into formalizing the Senior/PWD pool model. Kept as separate specs because they are independent subsystems (RBAC vs seat-inventory). Execution was direct-to-main via subagent-driven-development skill (no PR review gate).

**How to apply:** Both specs are now closed and all load-bearing follow-ups wired. If user returns asking about Reserved Pools, the cascade is now exercised end-to-end (walk-in submit → pools mutate → admin sees updated KPI → approve commits → manifest/boarding/reports see effect). Only #4 (dateFilter unused) remains as cosmetic polish. Related: [[feedback-no-lang-switcher-admin]] (admin screens have no language switcher — confirmed honored in `AdminGovHospitalApprovalsScreen`).

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 07:28:17Z)**_

Pushed as `5b2e0b5`. Vercel will auto-deploy in ~30s.

**Mobile changes:**
- Below `md` (768px): card stack — each booking shows ref + status chip header, large passenger name, voyage block, two-col Agency/ID grid, full-width reason, officer/submitted footer, then full-width Approve/Reject (`h-10`) or "Voyage departed" lock row, plus rejection reason / approver line for terminal states.
- `md` and up: existing 10-column table preserved.
- Strategy badge → "Mobile First".

Check on phone at https://fandsbookingapp-mockup.vercel.app and let me know if anything still feels cramped.
